user_message: Review the current code changes (staged, unstaged, and untracked files) and provide prioritized findings.
assistant_summary: {"findings":[{"title":"[P1] Replace recursive parent ordering before long checkpoint chains overflow","body":"When a pull brings in a long linear checkpoint history, `_visit()` recurses once per ancestor. With this project’s checkpoint-per-commit flow, importing roughly 1000+ new checkpoints in one lineage will hit Python’s recursion limit and abort the entire pull with `RecursionError`. The previous implementation was iterative, so this failure mode is introduced by the new DFS and should be rewritten with an explicit stack or other iterative ordering.","confidence_score":0.92,"priority":1,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/home/teslamint/Workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/sync/coordinator.py","line_range":{"start":271,"end":278}}},{"title":"[P2] Use exact checkpoint existence when deciding to clear missing parents","body":"The new missing-parent fallback calls `get_checkpoint(conn, parent_id)`, but that helper intentionally does prefix matching. If the DB contains `cp-parent-v2` and the imported child references missing `cp-parent`, this check returns a false positive, `parent_id` is kept, and `create_checkpoint()` still fails the foreign-key insert because no checkpoint with the exact id exists. This only shows up when ids share prefixes, but the codebase already uses short human-readable ids in tests and CLI paths.","confidence_score":0.95,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/home/teslamint/Workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/sync/coordinator.py","line_range":{"start":298,"end":300}}}],"overall_correctness":"patch is incorrect","overall_explanation":"The new checkpoint import ordering fixes one FK issue, but it also introduces a hard failure on deep parent chains and a false-positive parent existence check when ids share prefixes. Either case can cause `perform_pull` to abort on valid-looking data.","overall_confidence_score":0.94}
